drill.name: JetStick Half-Backswing Speed Out Front
drill.description: Half backswing with the JetStick; intent is to make the audible 'whoosh' arrive as far in front of the lead foot as possible.
drill.when_why: Use after warm-up and SpeedBomber primer. Reinforces speed delivered past impact, not at the ball. CNS-friendly because reps are short.
drill.active_cue: Hear the whoosh past your foot
drill.description_note: Player can think 'release out front' internally; do not say 'cast the wrists' in the cue field.
drill.constraint_alternative: Move target audible whoosh marker (cone) 2 ft forward of lead foot — the constraint teaches the same lesson.
drill.feedback_schedule: Novice 50% (call whoosh hit/miss). Intermediate 33%. Advanced self-report.
drill.conflict: TPI 'ticking time bomb' (speed percentile > power percentile) — gate failed. Acute wrist/elbow pain.
drill.progression: Move to JetStick Two-Way Speed (hip-high to hip-high, 1×6).
drill.regression: Drop to SpeedBomber 50–70% rhythm; rebuild base before speed intent.
drill.proof_metric: TrackMan CHS up ≥2 mph at retest with no loss of smash factor.
